diagnostic exports limited to required fields only

must_detect.matched_text:
Regulatory context: FCA-authorised UK issuer; assessment performed under UK GDPR and UK MAR.
MAR assessment: no inside information or price-sensitive details were generated by or revealed in this incident, and no STOR was required.
PII scope: logs contained only system IDs and role mailboxes (e.g., ops-support@northbridgemeridian.example); no natural-person contact details were processed during the outage.
Cross-border transfer (intentional positive): post-restoration, a single diagnostic export to our processor CloudNimble EU S.à r.l. (LU) was executed under approved Data Export RQ-784.
International transfer controls: DPA Schedule 2 (International Transfers) and the UK Addendum to the EU SCCs applied; data were pseudonymised and fields truncated to labels.
Employment data context: HR onboarding form templates include labels such as “Name”, “National Insurance number”, and “Sort code”; templates only were accessed, with no completed forms or attachments.
DSAR: no data-subject access requests arose from this incident; the last closed request (DSAR-2026-017) was fulfilled via the secure portal on 03 May 2026.
Consent withdrawal: none were received in the period; marketing preference management remains opt-in by default per our Cookies and Consent Policy.
Erasure: no erasure requests were linked to this event; routine deletion remains policy-driven rather than incident-driven.
Retention and minimisation: log retention remains 90 days under Policy RC-5, with diagnostic exports limited to required fields only as recorded in DPIA DP-OPS-41.
Processor governance: CloudNimble EU S.à r.l. performance and sub-processor list were reviewed against the DPA; no changes required and no onward transfers occurred.
